title: AST/GOT  Detection Assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"au1cn","text":"Intended use:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2vsk3","text":"Kinetic system for Aspartate Aminotransferase (AST) or Glutamic oxalacetic trasminase (GOT) determination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ec7i3","text":"Teste Principle:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a20lt","text":"AST catalyzes specifically  the transfer of amino groups of asparctic acid to a Ketoglutarate yielding oxalacetate and Glutamate. The oxalacetate is reduced to malate  by the enzyme malate dehydrogenase (MDH) and  coenzyme NADH is oxidized NAD.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"53nb0","text":"The reduction of the absorbance at 340 nm, as consequence of NADH oxidation, is determined photometrically and is direct proportional to the AST activity in the sample.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"291bq","text":"Aminotransferase determination involves the following reactions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9u991","text":"L- Aspartate + Ketoglutarate ---(AST) → Oxalacetate + L-Glutamate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ug64","text":"Oxalacetate  + NADH ---(MDH)→  Malate + NAD","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1c0e","text":"Reagents are for “in vitro” diagnostic use. Use the reagents according to the working procedures for clinical laboratories.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5ueon","text":"Storage and stability - Unopened reagents, when stored at indicated temperature, are stable up to expiration date shown on the label.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"8igc6","text":"Spectrophotometer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6sk7c","text":"- Micropipettes and pipettes for measuring the stated volumes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1vugl","text":"- Water bath at temperature indicated under PROCEDURE.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ae5iq","text":"- Stopwatch.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"asg3i","text":"The reagents contain sodium azide as preservative. Avoid ingestion. In case of eyes contact, immediately flush eyes with plenty of water and get prevent azide accumulation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
